away.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Kiki Rice
players.injury_comment: The injury was previously considered a Grade 2 sprain, so Rice is trending in the right direction. According to Copeland, Rice will be re-evaluated in the coming weeks. Julie Allemand will likely see an uptick in minutes in the meantime.

players.name: Brittney Sykes
players.injury_comment: Sykes is dealing with a plantar fascia injury and will remain sidelined for the Tempo. In the meantime, Toronto will likely lean more on Julie Allemand and Kia Nurse.

injuries.name: Brittney Sykes

home.starter_source: live ESPN roster/stat/injury APIs; starters inferred by live minutes + production

players.name: Brionna Jones
players.injury_comment: Jones, who hasn't yet been cleared for 5-on-5 drills, continues to make significant progress in her rehab from right knee surgery. The star center still doesn't carry a firm timetable for a return, but it sounds like she'll have a chance to return at some point in early July. Until Jones is ready to return, Naz Hillmon should continue to play a big role in the frontcourt alongside Angel Reese.

players.name: Aaliyah Nye
players.injury_comment: Nye will miss a third straight game due to a left knee injury. Her next opportunity to suit up will come Wednesday against Golden State.
